candidate_surfaces.selection_reason: Runtime adoption is the next smallest lawful surface after the reuse-authority boundary held: it can expose typed runtime halts and failure modes without pre-authorizing any build, probe, or rerun.
candidate_surfaces.surface_question: After the reuse-authority boundary held and surface-selection reentry was accepted, what is the smallest runtime-adoption surface that can expose typed halts, receipt mismatches, projection bugs, missing moves, or gate failures without pre-authorizing build, probe execution, C8 rerun, or reusable schema promotion?

candidate_surfaces.selection_reason: Important but secondary; best opened from concrete runtime-observed failures rather than before runtime reentry.
candidate_surfaces.surface_question: What minimum feedback-hardening surface would make failed units report why, where, relative to what object, and what refinement would allow progress?

candidate_surfaces.selection_reason: Relevant due the repaired report projection bug, but narrower than the runtime-adoption surface.
candidate_surfaces.surface_question: What minimum projection-hygiene surface would reduce report/readout mismatch without expanding authority?

selected_surface_question: After the reuse-authority boundary held and surface-selection reentry was accepted, what is the smallest runtime-adoption surface that can expose typed halts, receipt mismatches, projection bugs, missing moves, or gate failures without pre-authorizing build, probe execution, C8 rerun, or reusable schema promotion?
